title: Home
summary: Single primary discovery affordance, author identity secondary, brand identity tertiary. Differs by thesis (Thesis A = featured-piece-first; Thesis B = current-Dispatch-first).

strategy.decision: Work-first ordering (Rule 3): the latest substantive piece or the current Dispatch sits above the fold. Author identity is second; brand mark is third. Reverses v1's brand-first lead and rejects the peer-set's author-first default.
strategy.why: Rule 3 (Stage 2). Competitive synthesis showed 5/5 peer publications lead with author identity; v1 leads with publication name; both miss what the hiring-evaluator persona is looking for. Hiring evaluators arrive to test whether the work is real, not to admire the byline or the brand. Work-first is the only ordering that serves that arrival.
strategy.shipped: Current state at ninochavez.co/blog leads with publication name + brand bar, then hero/featured post, with three right-rail panels ('Currently Surfacing', 'Recent Series', 'Also Here') competing for attention. See v1-baseline/01-diagnose.md F5.
strategy.gap: v2 cuts the home page to one primary discovery surface above the fold (featured or current Dispatch), with format-badged recent items below. Right-rail panels removed; their content moves to Library and About surfaces.
strategy.question: Thesis A (positioning realignment) or Thesis B (different publication)? Determines whether 'the work' is 'the latest substantive piece' or 'the current Dispatch #N.' Logged as D1 — blocks this page's build.

breaksFromV1: primary nav reduced from 5+ items to 3 (Rule 4)
author identity surfaced above the fold (new affordance)

currentState.summary: v1 home: brand bar, featured hero, post grid, 3-panel right rail. Multiple competing affordances above the fold.
currentState.annotation: v2 collapses to one primary discovery affordance, surfaces author identity above the fold, demotes brand mark.
